findings.description: The UserForm constructor `public UserForm(Map<String, Object> params)` is omitted from the knowledge file's code example. The source shows `user = (UserEntity) params.get("user"); address = (AddressEntity) params.get("address");` inside this constructor, which illustrates how child Forms are extracted from request parameters — part of the complete implementation pattern.
findings.source_evidence: Source: `public UserForm(Map<String, Object> params) { user = (UserEntity) params.get("user"); address = (AddressEntity) params.get("address"); }`

findings.description: `INSERT_PARAMS` is referenced in the UserEntity code example (`ValidationUtil.validate(context, INSERT_PARAMS)`) but its definition is omitted. The source defines it as `private static final String[] INSERT_PARAMS = new String[] {"name", "remarks"};`, which is required for the code example to be self-contained and correct.
findings.source_evidence: Source: `private static final String[] INSERT_PARAMS = new String[] {"name", "remarks"};`

findings.description: The automated warning (section count 3 < source headings 7) is not a real issue.
